title: Step 2: Confirm the database to delete.

sections.heading: Required Roles
sections.body:
The following table describes the roles required to perform
various actions to a database in the Atlas UI:

| Action           | Required Roles                                                                                                        |
| ---------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Create Databases | One of the following roles:Project Owner or Organization OwnerProject Data Access AdminProject Data Access Read/Write |
| View Databases   | At least the Project Data Access Read Only role.                                                                      |
| Drop Databases   | One of the following roles:Project OwnerProject Data Access Admin                                                     |

sections.heading: Create a Database
sections.body:
To create a database through the Atlas UI:

- If it's not already displayed, select the
  organization that contains your project from the
  Organizations menu in the navigation bar.

- If it's not already displayed, select your project
  from the Projects menu in the navigation bar.

- In the sidebar, click Data Explorer under
  the Database heading.

  The [Data Explorer](https://cloud.mongodb.com/go?l=https%3A%2F%2Fcloud.mongodb.com%2Fv2%2F%3Cproject%3E%23%2Fmetrics%2FreplicaSet%2F%3Creplset%3E%2Fexplorer) displays.

In the Connections sidebar, select or hover over your cluster
and click the  icon to open the Create Database
dialog box.

Enter the Database Name and the Collection
Name to create the database and its first collection.

If you want to use [custom collation](manual/reference/collation) on the collection,
select the Use Custom Collation checkbox and select the
desired collation settings.

> **Important:** Don't include [sensitive information](sensitive-info) in
> your database and collection names.

For more information on MongoDB database names and collection names,
see [Naming Restrictions](manual/reference/limits).

Select whether the collection is a
[time series collection](manual/core/timeseries-collections).
If you select to create a time series collection, specify the time
field and granularity. You can optionally specify the meta field and
the time for old data in the collection to expire.

Upon successful creation, the database and the collection appears in
the Connections sidebar.

sections.heading: View Databases
sections.body: To view the databases and collections in the deployment:

sections.heading: Visualize Database Data
sections.body: To launch MongoDB Charts to visualize data in your databases and collections:

sections.heading: Step 1: Launch .
sections.body:
To visualize data in MongoDB Charts from the Atlas UI, click
Visualize Your Data when viewing a specific database
or collection. Charts loads the data source and you can
start building a chart in the Charts view. For detailed
steps, see [Build Charts](charts/build-charts).

sections.heading: Drop a Database
sections.body:
To drop a database, including all its collections, through the
Atlas UI:

In the Connections sidebar, select or hover over the database
to drop and click on the trash can icon. A confirmation dialog appears.

Confirm by typing the name of the database, and click
Drop Database.
